stages.label: Foundation
stages.blurb: Identity + tooling — the prerequisite for everything else.

stages.label: Core loop
stages.blurb: The PR-driven control plane that every other challenge extends.

stages.label: Governed control plane
stages.blurb: Items, domains, labels, RBAC — the front door of governance.

stages.label: Intelligent governance
stages.blurb: Medallion, agents, observability, promotion — scale with guardrails.

stages.label: Capstone & evolve
stages.blurb: Integrate the team's chosen subset end-to-end, then compound it.

challenges.name: Setup
challenges.tagline: Tenant, identity, and tooling are wired up. The agent can talk to Fabric.
challenges.outcome: Fabric tenant ready for RVAS, SPN federated to GitHub via OIDC, MCP + Skills installed and verified.
challenges.value: Zero-secret automation; identity that auditors can defend.

challenges.name: Workspace as Code
challenges.tagline: Nothing exists in the tenant unless a YAML manifest for it lives in main.
challenges.outcome: Workspaces created, updated, and decommissioned exclusively via Pull Requests. Drift detected nightly.
challenges.value: Auditable workspace lifecycle; provable "no ClickOps" posture.

challenges.name: Items as Code
challenges.tagline: Governance reaches inside the workspace: every lakehouse, notebook, warehouse is declared.
challenges.outcome: Item manifests with per-kind naming, description, owner, and sensitivity rules. Validator + provisioner extended.
challenges.value: No more "mystery lakehouses"; column-naming and secret-hygiene rules enforced at PR time.

challenges.name: Domains, Capacities, Sensitivity
challenges.tagline: Every workspace lands in the right domain, on the right capacity, with the right label.
challenges.outcome: Domains and capacities as code; sensitivity labels applied at provision time; label-promotion review flow.
challenges.value: Chargeback accuracy, Purview-aligned classification, region/capacity allow-lists.

challenges.name: Access & RBAC Lifecycle
challenges.tagline: Group-first, expiring assignments, JIT break-glass, quarterly access reviews.
challenges.outcome: Role assignments declared in access/; quarterly review workflow auto-opens PRs; break-glass paper-trail.
challenges.value: Role sprawl eliminated; access reviews become a PR, not a spreadsheet.

challenges.name: Medallion Bootstrap
challenges.tagline: One PR scaffolds a fully governed Bronze / Silver / Gold lakehouse set.
challenges.outcome: Per-tier sensitivity floors, endorsement, owners, schedules — produced by the e2e-medallion-architecture skill and captured back into manifests.
challenges.value: Days-to-hours for new medallion stand-up; governance baked in, not bolted on.

challenges.name: Data Agent Governance
challenges.tagline: Natural-language Q&A agents, but only over allow-listed sources with RAI-linted instructions.
challenges.outcome: Agent manifests gate data sources by sensitivity, RAI-lint catches refusal/scope gaps, drift detects out-of-band instruction edits.
challenges.value: Business-user self-service Q&A without the data-exfiltration tail risk.

challenges.name: Audit & Observability
challenges.tagline: Every governance event lands in an Eventhouse, surfaces in Power BI, trips Activator alerts.
challenges.outcome: KQL governance store, near-real-time ingestion of GitHub + Fabric activity, "Governance Health" dashboard, anomaly reflexes.
challenges.value: Governance you can see; SLOs around PR throughput, drift, label coverage.

challenges.name: Deployment Pipelines
challenges.tagline: dev → stg → prd promotion via Fabric pipelines, gated by PR labels and the same environment approval.
challenges.outcome: Pipelines as code; label-driven promotion; revert/prd rollback flow with a post-mortem PR.
challenges.value: Repeatable, auditable production releases; rollback in minutes, not meetings.

challenges.name: Capstone — End-to-end integration
challenges.tagline: One PR (or short stacked series) exercises every challenge the team completed.
challenges.outcome: Realistic business scenario shipped through the governance plane; demo of artifacts vs. rubric.
challenges.value: Proof, on the team's own data, that governance does not slow delivery — it accelerates it.
